title: Kanecta Config
description:
Bootstrap configuration for the Kanecta platform. Tells the system where datastores live and how to connect to remotes. Not a Kanecta item — exists one layer below the datastore.

Discovery order (every entry point — Electron, npm start, MCP, API — uses the same algorithm):
  1. KANECTA_CONFIG environment variable — if set, use that path and stop.
  2. Platform default:
     - Mac:     ~/Library/Application Support/kanecta/config.json
     - Linux:   ~/.config/kanecta/config.json  (XDG)
     - Windows: %APPDATA%\kanecta\config.json

Secret values (passwords, API keys) belong in a .env file beside config.json, not in config.json itself. String values beginning with '$' are resolved from environment variables at runtime (e.g. '$KANECTA_DB_PASSWORD').

specVersion.description: Version of the Kanecta config schema this file conforms to (e.g. '1.4.0').

defaultWorkingSet.description: Name of the workingSet to use when none is specified. Must be a key in 'workingSets'.

workingSets.description: Named workingSet definitions. Each workingSet is a local datastore with optional remote connections.

studioPort.description: Port for the Kanecta Studio UI server. Defaults to 9743 if absent.

apiPort.description: Port for the Kanecta API server. Defaults to 9744 if absent.

local.description: Local datastore. Either a filesystem path (string shorthand) or a typed local adapter definition.

oneOf.description: Shorthand: absolute path to a filesystem datastore directory.

remotes.description: Named remote connections. 'origin' is the conventional default, mirroring Git. Multiple remotes are supported.

defaultBranch.description: Fallback branch for this working set, used when no active branch is recorded in state.json and none is supplied via KANECTA_BRANCH or a per-call argument. Defaults to 'main'. Each adapter maps the branch name to its native mechanism: filesystem → branch folder, Postgres → schema name (slashes become double-underscores), S3 → key prefix. Kanecta branching is independent of Git.

features.description: Optional feature flags for this workingSet. Omitting a flag is equivalent to false.

history.description: Enable item_history tracking. When true, every item write produces an item_history item capturing the event type, a field-level delta, and a full snapshot. Disabled by default. For filesystem workingSets tracked by Git, leave this false — Git already provides history and enabling it would double up and conflict with Git's in-place change model. For Postgres workingSets without Git, consider enabling. When disabled, the system is fully functional — history is not required for any core feature.

activity.description: Enable activity tracking. When true, application-level events (item.viewed, search.performed, sync.completed, etc.) are recorded as activity items. Disabled by default. Activity can grow extremely large in high-traffic workingSets — a single popular item can generate millions of activity items. Enable only when the activity data will be actively used. When disabled, the system is fully functional.

path.description: Absolute path to the local filesystem datastore directory.

host.description: Postgres host. Supports '$VAR' env var resolution.

port.description: Postgres port. Defaults to 5432. Supports '$VAR' env var resolution.

database.description: Database name. Supports '$VAR' env var resolution.

user.description: Database user. Supports '$VAR' env var resolution.

password.description: Database password. Use '$VAR' env var reference — do not hardcode secrets.

remote.description: A remote datastore connection. Type determines the required fields. String values beginning with '$' are resolved from environment variables at runtime.

host.description: Postgres host. Supports '$VAR' env var resolution.

port.description: Postgres port. Defaults to 5432. Supports '$VAR' env var resolution.

database.description: Database name. Supports '$VAR' env var resolution.

user.description: Database user. Supports '$VAR' env var resolution.

password.description: Database password. Use '$VAR' env var reference — do not hardcode secrets.

path.description: Absolute path to the SQLite database file. Supports '$VAR' env var resolution.

url.description: Base URL of the Kanecta server (e.g. 'https://kanecta.acme.com').

apiKey.description: API key for authentication. Use '$VAR' env var reference — do not hardcode secrets.
